title: Etsy Inc (ETSY) Q4 2024 Earnings Call Highlights: Record Revenue Amidst GMS Decline
text: Etsy reported record revenue of $2.8 billion for 2024, marking a 2% year-over-year increase, though this positive result masks underlying challenges. The company achieved adjusted EBITDA of $782 million with a 27.8% margin, demonstrating operational strength. For the fourth quarter specifically, revenue reached $852 million, up 1.2% year-over-year, with adjusted EBITDA of $251 million, reflecting a robust 29.4% margin. A critical concern emerged: consolidated gross merchandise sales (GMS) declined 4% to $12.6 billion annually. Q4 performance was particularly weak, with core marketplace GMS dropping 8.6% year-over-year to approximately $3.7 billion. CEO Josh Silverman acknowledged that the company anticipates a continued decline in GMS for the first quarter of 2025, signaling ongoing headwinds. Results varied significantly across Etsy's portfolio: Depop (fashion resale) surged nearly 32% year-over-year to $789 million; Reverb (music marketplace) declined 2.6% to $918 million. Management outlined two critical initiatives for 2025: enhancing customer experience through improved search algorithms and rebalancing product development to address near-term GMS recovery.
